what_it_does: Acceptance test for L4 located gold labels: it verifies that an addressed ui:// comment submitted through Suite.ingest_comment (and composed through Suite.route_click) records the I6 annotation and also emits a gold/operator chat turn carrying the same address, that the turn appears in training_signal with the address intact, that training_signal's existing operator-gold/echo-guard filter remains unchanged, that direct Suite.annotate stays pure and writes no chat history, and that non-operator comments cannot launder into gold.
observations: The file is mostly inline documentation explaining the §21.7#7 design decision and test strategy.
It tests both Suite.ingest_comment and Suite.route_click as real entry points for located-gold emission.
It greps runtime/bridge.py to verify that POST /api/annotate dispatches ingest_comment and does not call the pure annotate() leaf.
It uses a temporary FsStore, sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1, and cleans up with shutil.rmtree.
It includes negative cases: a twin/assistant addressed turn is filtered from training_signal, and a source='twin' comment cannot become gold.
The only deterministic symbol is a small check() helper that asserts and counts passing checks.
standouts: The bridge reachability check slices the /api/annotate handler block from bridge.py and asserts the dispatch target inside it, which is an unusual source-level acceptance guard.
The test explicitly frames ingest_comment as the wired production entry and annotate() as a pure leaf, with a hard-preserve rationale.
It asserts that every training_signal turn remains operator-gold, treating the filter as byte-for-byte unchanged.
conventions: Uses a global PASS counter and a custom check(label, cond) assertion helper.
Uses tempfile.mkdtemp and a finally cleanup block.
Imports FsStore, NodeRegistry, and Suite from their runtime modules.
Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN for inbox-hygiene parity with sibling suites.
concerns: The test parses bridge.py source at runtime, making it brittle to handler formatting or route ordering changes.
The global PASS counter and side-effecting check() helper are not thread-safe or pytest-idiomatic.
It calls internal-looking methods such as training_signal and _provenance_grade, coupling the test to private implementation details.
Hard-coded relative paths and assumptions about node discovery may fail outside the expected repository layout.
notes: Acceptance test that locks located-gold emission to the wired ingest_comment entry point while guarding the pure annotate() leaf and the existing training-signal filter.
questions: What are the exact signatures and contracts of Suite.ingest_comment and Suite.route_click in the implementation?
Is _provenance_grade intended to be a stable public method, or is it an internal helper?
How does the test prove the training_signal filter is byte-for-byte unchanged rather than merely producing the same observable behavior?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual: it documents and verifies that addressed operator comments become located gold labels via ingest_comment while preserving I6 separation and existing filters.
apparent_themes: Separation between the I6 annotation leaf and the I5 router/wired entry
Echo-guard and source filtering in training data
End-to-end acceptance testing with negative guards
intention_signals: Encode a design decision: located gold must be emitted at the wired comment-ingest layer (ingest_comment, route_click, and bridge /api/annotate), not in the low-level annotate() leaf, so that existing separation tests and training filters remain intact.
novelty: The located-gold concept: a ui:// address attached to an operator comment rides through append_chat and training_signal as a spatially-located gold label without modifying the training filter.
contribution: A concrete acceptance specification and guard for how addressed operator comments become located gold training turns, including negative cases that protect against twin turns or non-operator sources entering the gold training signal.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test verifying that addressed operator comments via Suite.ingest_comment emit gold/operator chat turns carrying a ui:// address, flow into training_signal as located gold labels, and preserve the existing training filter and the pure annotate() leaf.
intention_for_embedding: Lock the L4 located-gold behavior to the wired ingest_comment entry point and protect the I6 separation and training-signal filter invariants.

symbols.does: Test helper that asserts a condition and increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) is a lightweight assertion helper used throughout the acceptance test. It asserts cond with a labeled failure message and increments the global PASS counter on success, printing an ok line for each passing check.
